title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs timing, resource graphs, transfer, caching, and user-performance boundaries from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded page-resource and timing audit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Separate queue, DNS, connect, TLS, request, first-byte, body, parse, render, and total timing with clock and instrumentation limits.
Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
Reconcile compressed transfer, decoded body, cached reuse, duplicate URLs, variant keys, and large-resource policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: User Performance Boundary case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 3598 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate crawler timings, controlled browser lab evidence, field data, accessibility, network profile, device profile, and user experience claims. Artifact under review: bounded page-resource and timing audit.

options.text: The User Performance Boundary record says bounded page-resource and timing audit looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for User Performance Boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy User Performance Boundary for bounded page-resource and timing audit.
The record names the assumption, applies User Performance Boundary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
